rulings.Case Title: JANICE MENDENHALL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (D.H.S)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Requesting An Order For Settlement On Deft'S
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 9, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JANICE MENDENHALL Motion Requesting An Order For Settlement On Defendant's. The motion is denied. It is unclear what relief plaintiff seeks and what is the statutory or legal basis for the motion. If plaintiff is seeking reconsideration of the court's prior rulings, the motion is untimely. The court lacks the power to reconsider those prior rulings because judgments in favor of the defendants have been entered. See Ramon v. Aerospace Corp. (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 1233, 1236. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SHELLY CARPER et al VS. ADKNOWLEDGE INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 9, 2015, Line 14, DEFENDANT ADKNOWLEDGE INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication. Motion denied. First, Defendant produced no authority for its contention that Plaintiffs' act of sharing access of the subject email account in some way divested them of ownership of the account, and therefore has not negated the "recipient" element of Plaintiffs' claim. Second, Defendant has failed to produce evidence to support its contention that the sender of the emails could be identified in the body of the emails. While the parties do not dispute that the emails contained a link to the sender's website (UMF # 7), in contrast to Rosolowski v. Guthy-Renker LLC (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 1403, there is no evidence that the sender was identifiable by the link itself, that there was an unsubscribe link in the emails, or that the sender's physical address was contained in the emails. While Defendant asserted that the emails contain these elements, the evidence submitted in support fails to establish the assertion. Thus, Defendant has not shown as a matter of law that it is entitled to judgment. Plaintiffs' claims are not preempted by the federal CAN-SPAM act. (See Hypertouch, Inc. v. ValueClick, Inc. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 805, 825, 832-33.) Finally, Defendant has produced insufficient evidence for the Court to determine whether it has practices and procedures in place that meet the statutory standard for a reduction in the maximum damages permitted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SHELLY CARPER et al VS. ADKNOWLEDGE INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To 152 Spams
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 9, 2015, Line 14, PLAINTIFF SHELLY CARPER, STEVE CARPER MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To 152 Spams. Motion denied. Plaintiffs have failed to produce any admissible evidence establishing that the emails at issue originated from proxy-registered domain names, and thus, Plaintiffs have not established that the emails were untraceable using public online databases. The evidence submitted in the declarations of Jamie Carper and Jacob Harker lack authentication. (See Sole Energy Co. v. Petrominerals Corp. (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 187, 195 [authentication rules apply to computer printouts].) Plaintiffs presented numerous pages that appeared to be printouts of websites found at Internet addresses for the proxy companies. Plaintiffs also submitted declarations stating that these pages were "true and correct copies of web pages from" the respective websites. But Plaintiffs did not present any evidence showing the websites were in fact created by the companies or that the statements on the printouts were made by persons authorized to speak for them. This information was essential to authenticate the information as statements of the proxy companies-i.e., that the documents were what they purported to be. (See Evid. Code § 1400.) Nor is there any exception permitted due to "self-authentication" because although the printouts may be accurate copies of what was on the computer screen, Plaintiffs were also required to present evidence that the information was prepared and generated by the proxy companies. (See People v. Hawkins (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 1428, 1450 ["presumption [in § 1552] operates to establish only that a computer's print function has worked properly. The presumption does not operate to establish the accuracy or reliability of the printed information."].) The same is true of the WHOIS reports relied upon by Plaintiffs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JUSTIN MOORE VS. RICHARD BURDEN TEED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 9, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT HOM-PISANO ENGINEERING, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary adjudication is denied because it is procedurally defective under CCP 437c(f)(1). First, the motion is improper because a claim for damages under CCP 437c(f)(1) refers to a claim for punitive damages. See Catalano v. Sup. Crt., (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 91, 96-97; DeCastro West Chodorow & Burns, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 410, 419. Second, any ruling regarding the limitation of liability clause does not completely dispose of a damages claim. D's Notice of Motion cites CAZA Drilling (CA) Inc. v. TEG Oil & Gas USA, Inc. (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 453, 471-472 and Burnett v. Chimney Sweep (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 1057, 1065. Those cases discuss when an exculpatory clause can be enforceable. Those cases do not discuss the procedural summary adjudication issue. Defendant also cites Markborough California, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (1991) 227 Cal.App.3d 705, 708. In that case, the lower court granted summary adjudication on the limitation of liability issue. The Markborough court does not discuss the summary adjudication procedural issue and it appears that the decision predates amendments made to CCP 437c(f)(1). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: D CAMERON BAKER VS. PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES FOUNDATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Precluding Disclosure And Dissemination Of Confidential Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 9, 2015, line 1. DEFENDANT PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES FOUNDATION'S Motion For Protective Order Precluding Disclosure And Dissemination Of Confidential Documents. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion for a Protective Order is denied without prejudice to it making a showing as to specific documents which it asserts should be made subject to confidentiality. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: D CAMERON BAKER VS. PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES FOUNDATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant D. Cameron Bakers Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Against Pwdf
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 9, 2015, line 1. Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant D. Cameron Baker's Motion To Compel Further Responses And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Against PWDF. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel is granted. Defendant will produce all documents responsive to requests nos. 1-9 within ten (10) days from the date of this Order. To the extent that Defendant is withholding any responsive document based on a claim of privilege, it will describe the document with sufficient particularity to enable Plaintiff to challenge the claim. In addition, if Defendant asserts that it is unable to produce a document based upon the order of the U.S. District Court it shall describe that document and explain why its contends are covered by that Order. The Complaint seeks money damages for an alleged breach of contract to pay a bonus in legal fees based upon recoveries in the actions filed in the U.S. District Court. All of the requested documents relate to that claim and should have been produced without controversy. Defendant was not justified in refusing to produce any document without a protective order. Monetary sanctions will be assessed in the amount of $3,000.00. Counsel for Plaintiff should prepare an appropriate order, submit to counsel for Defendant for approval as to form, and deliver it to the Court at the hearing on February 9, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY CLARK et al VS. SEGWAY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 9, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT CHARLES KEES, CHARLES KEES DBA SEGWAY SAN FRANCISCO BAY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e) and (f).) As to the second cause of action for strict product liability, the demurrer is sustained because the complaint does not allege that Defendant rented new Segways to its customers. (See Wilkinson v. Hicks (1981) 126 Cal.App.3d 515, 519; Arriaga v. CitiCapital Commercial Corp. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 1527, 1541.) As to the third cause of action for fraud, the demurrer is sustained because the complaint does not adequately allege knowledge of falsity or intent to defraud. As to the fourth cause of action for fraud in the inducement, the demurrer is sustained because the contract and waiver agreement is not attached to the complaint and therefore the cause of action is uncertain. (Alameda County Title Ins. Co. v. Panella (1933) 218 Cal. 510, 513; FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 383.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The need for this Motion appears to have been rendered moot by the further responses filed by Cross-Defendant on December 18, 2014. In her moving papers, Cross-Complainant makes no reference to those supplemental responses nor does she argue that they are inadequate requiring further action by the court. No monetary sanctions will be assessed. Counsel for Cross-Defendant will prepare an order denying Cross-Complainant's motion on the grounds of mootness, submit to counsel for Cross-Complainant for approval as to form and deliver to the Court for signing at the hearing on February 9, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
